repo_catalog.purpose: Core desktop/runtime repo and canonical Pulse v6 release-control authority.
Closed-source enterprise and paid runtime features that should not ship as open-source surfaces.
Mobile client, mobile relay flows, approval UX, and device-local auth/state handling.
Financial, operational, checkout, license-server, and relay-server surfaces that back commercial flows.

readiness.repo_ready_rule: all lanes target-met and evidence-present plus all repo-ready assertions passed
readiness.rc_ready_rule: repo_ready plus all rc-ready assertions passed plus zero rc-ready open_decisions plus all rc-ready release_gates passed
readiness.release_ready_rule: rc_ready plus all release-ready assertions passed plus zero release-ready open_decisions plus all release-ready release_gates passed

readiness_assertions.summary: Governed surfaces that should use the unified resource model do not keep shipping on legacy equivalents.
New users can complete Pulse Pro Relay signup through paid activation without manual operator intervention or ambiguous provisioning steps.
After first successful entitlement activation, Pulse preserves paid state across supported sessions and upgrades without repeated license entry.
Typical end users cannot trivially unlock Pulse Pro features by stripping client-only checks while server and hosted entitlements still deny access.
Non-paid users do not see paid-only navigation or pages unless the surface is explicitly promotional.
Supported upgrades preserve core state, entitlements, and first-session continuity without manual repair steps.

Agent allocation stays coherent across enforcement, entitlements, and UI: only installed v6 agents count toward max_agents, capped limits stay canonical, and users see the same active-agent usage that the runtime enforces.
Stable or GA promotion happens only after an exercised RC, live release-pipeline proof, a recorded rollback target, and a written v5 maintenance-only support policy.

A real v5-installed Pulse Unified Agent upgrades through v6 release assets into one canonical v6 agent identity, preserves one-shot update continuity metadata, keeps legacy persisted host-agent token scopes valid at the v6 canonical agent endpoints, and does not drift agent-count or fallback behavior during the crossover.
Active Pulse v6 guidance reflects the current governed behavior of the codebase, and legacy or historical documentation does not remain part of the active v6 guidance surface.

Pulse Hosted works as a real offered v6 tier: customers can sign up or sign in to a hosted tenant, enter a working hosted runtime, and use hosted billing/admin surfaces without self-hosted fallback paths or broken post-provisioning behavior.

Multi-tenant Pulse is sound: tenant isolation, organization membership, RBAC scope, tenant-scoped runtime state, sharing, and migration all fail closed outside the intended tenant boundary instead of partially behaving like a single-tenant system.

release_gates.summary: Confirm API tokens are assigned to the correct user and org context, enforce scope boundaries including legacy persisted host-agent scope aliases, and revoke cleanly.
Confirm the actual hosted Pulse tier works end to end after provisioning: hosted auth, runtime entry, and hosted billing/admin surfaces all function coherently.
Confirm grandfathered v5 recurring continuity holds while active, completed cancellation revokes paid state, and re-entry after cancellation uses current public v6 pricing.
Confirm active v6-facing guidance is current, and any legacy or historical docs are clearly archived or demoted instead of remaining current guidance.
Confirm hosted signup, org provisioning, billing-admin visibility, and webhook replay succeed in the real checkout path.
Confirm pulse-mobile pairing, persistence, relay reconnect, auth transitions, and approval flows work against a real instance.
Confirm multi-tenant Pulse behaves as a coherent tenant-isolated product across org scope, runtime state, sharing, and migration.
Confirm user creation, organization membership, RBAC scope, and cross-org sharing fail closed outside intended access.
Confirm free-versus-paid feature gating and agent allocation accounting match entitlements across UI, API, hosted, and upgrade surfaces.
Confirm stable or GA promotion is coming from an exercised RC with live release-pipeline proof, rollback instructions, and a written v5 maintenance-only policy.
Confirm relay fresh registration, reconnect, stale-session recovery, and disconnect drain behavior hold under real pressure.

Confirm a real v5-installed Pulse Unified Agent upgrades through candidate v6 RC assets into one canonical v6 agent identity without duplicate registration, stale fallback or legacy-scope breakage, or agent-count drift.
Confirm supported upgrades preserve state, entitlements, and first-session continuity without repair flows.

open_decisions.summary: Pulse Mobile still needs a clearer usefulness floor for runtime navigation, relay-state feedback, and approval recovery before v6 RC stabilization can be considered complete.

resolved_decisions.summary: Staged release policy locked; L4 Hosted MSP full portal is post-GA and not a GA floor gate.
MSP pricing locked at Starter/Growth/Scale = $149/$249/$399 monthly with 10/25/50 workspace breakpoints.
Cloud/MSP Stripe mapping contract is locked; concrete price IDs remain operational fill-in tasks.
Host-type migration audit completed; remaining host references are compatibility boundaries, internal shims, or non-resource terminology.
Trial authority for v6 is SaaS-controlled; hosted signup initiates trial start and local runtime may only redeem signed trial activations.
The v5-to-v6 license migration bridge landed, including upgrade exchange, activation input acceptance, and aligned public migration guidance.
Browser-level proof exists for unresolved v5 commercial migration states via the upgraded-fixture commercial migration spec.
The v5 commercial migration truth table is owned by v6 release-control and unresolved paid-license states must remain preserved through migration.
Embedded frontend drift protection landed through synced dist assets, embed parity tests, and native embedded migration UI proof.
The v6 release-control orchestrator was retired; direct repo-aware sessions and governance guardrails are the only supported execution path.
SOURCE_OF_TRUTH.md now owns only stable governance and locked decisions; live lane state and evidence references live only in status.json.

v6 and later releases use an opt-in RC preview channel plus a promotion-only stable channel; stable customers must receive only already-validated builds, unattended auto-update exposure remains stable-only, and GA stays blocked until rollback instructions and the v5 maintenance-only policy are explicit.

Once v6 reaches stable or GA, v5 moves to a 90-day maintenance-only window for critical security issues, critical correctness/data-loss issues, and safe migration blockers only; after that window, v5 is unsupported.

Paid Pulse Pro v5 customers keep their existing recurring price through the v6 pricing change until they cancel; renewals must preserve that grandfathered price state, while any return after cancellation re-enters on current v6 pricing.
The 13 canonical Cloud/MSP v6 price IDs are populated in the governed pulse-pro operations doc, launch checklist, and license-server env mapping template.
The 13 canonical Cloud/MSP v6 price IDs already exist as active live recurring Stripe prices and match the governed commercial mappings.
